calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-02-2024 Granted
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann appeared by zoom for Wille Faye Garrett.

Mr. Vann stated that he has not heard from Mrs. Davis-Jenkins and that she has been noticed of today's hearing. The court noted the amount of time that was spent by Mr. Vann in this case and that he is willing to the cash on hand ($5,486.00) as full payment of his services. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to allow (but not approve) the final report including preliminary cash distribution outright to the devisees without prior court approval; grant the request to distribute the remaining estate real property in Nice, Lake County to the devisees in equal shares per their agreement overriding difference shares specified in the codicil; and waive the codicil requirement to create a testamentary trust to hold Winston Hearring's cash share because there will be no estate cash left after payment of the attorneys' statutory fees to fund the trust.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Dennis Vann, Esq., for Willie Garrett, Executor.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to May-01-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Vann is instructed to file his declaration by Apr-24-2024.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-07-2020 Continued To Jan-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hannah Oxley appeared by zoom for Petitioner.

The Court noted that at the last calling of the matter it asked for a title report of the Bridgeview Drive property. Ms. Oxley stated that she tried to file a status report and a title report on Monday, that it got rejected and that she re-filed it on Tuesday. Ms. Oxley also stated that there are five holders of the property as Tenants-in-Common, that she believes the property is out of this estate and that there is no right of survivorship although the four other property holders are heirs. In addition, Ms. Oxley stated that she believes two of the property holders have passed away since Ms. Hearring's death, that the other remaining property holders are wishing to pass ownership to one of the heirs of Ms. Hearring, and that she believes the next step is getting the house appraised and getting the heirs on board to executing some documents. Furthermore, Ms. Oxley stated that the Executor does not live in the property and that she believes a grandchild of the Decedent lives there. The Court Clerk informed Ms. Oxley that her filings of November 30th and December 2nd were both rejected. The Court advised Ms. Oxley to re-title her document and not to include a hearing date. Status on close of estate is continued to January 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-19-2020 Continued To Dec-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Hannah Oxley appeared by zoom for Willie Garrett (Petitioner).
Janet Boessenecker appeared by zoom for Public Administrator.

Ms. Oxley stated that they filed a status report and a motion to be relieved as Counsel, that it is impossible to communicate with Willie, that continuing to represent Willie is a violation of their ethical responsibility, and requested that this matter be put on pause until the motion to be relieved is heard. Ms. Boessenecker questions if this is even a probate estate/whether it meets the threshold of probate, stated that the real property mentioned appears to be the residence of the Administrator or a family member, that there is a 2013 document that shows conveyance of the 258 Bridgeview Drive real property and that she is unable to see the deed. Ms. Oxley stated that it's a joint tenancy deed between five people and that this is new information. Ms. Oxley stated that the Fresno land sold for $25,000.00 or $30,000.00, that they cannot sell the Clear Lake property and that it is only worth $5,000.00. The Court stated that it needs a title report on the Bridgeview property. Status on close of estate is continued to December 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to submit a title report and to make a finding that the property is not in the estate. Said documents are to be filed one week prior to the hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: H. Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hannah B. Oxley, Esq., counsel for Willie Garrett, executor - by telephone.

At the Court's request, Ms. Oxley reports on the status of the administration of the estate, noting that the last matter to be resolved is the sale of undeveloped real estate in Clear Lake, California. Ms. Oxley further notes that there has been some difficulty marketing the property for sale due to the current health concern but opines that the property should be offered for sale shortly and the status of the same known within the next 60 to 90 days. The Court continues the matter to October 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. for further status of the administration or for the filing of a Petition for Final Distribution. If the Petition is on file before October 19, 2020, no appearance will be required and the matter will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputies Choi and Nuque Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Pierson on behalf of attorney Patanisha Davis-Jenkins for Willie Garrett, Petitioner, via Court Call.

Mr. Pierson stated that the client has not been responsive to communication, that his office received a message there may be a buyer for the 2 lots in Clear Lake and that he has not had a chance to talk to the client. The Court noted that this is the fourth hearing to get a petition on status of administration or close of estate, and it needs a petition on status on close.

The status on close of estate is continued to April 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Courtesy copies to be delivered one week prior. The Court is inclined to suspend or remove the administrator if it does not receive anything.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone Willie Garrett.

Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that there were some plots that needed to be sold and some land, that at the last hearing she informed the Court that she was going to substitute out of the case because there was no communication from the Client, that she received information that her Client may not have gotten back to her because she was having health issues and that she was informed that the plots may not belong to the estate. Ms. Davis-Jenkins also stated that the land is in Clearlake and that she was told by her Client to communicate with the Attorney who is handling the Clearlake property. Status on close of estate is continued to March 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar. The Court informed Ms. Davis-Jenkins that she needs to file a second petition for approval of a report on status of administration, or a petition for final distribution.

Appearances:
Patanisha Davis-Jenkins for Petitioner via Court Call.

Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that she did submit 1st and final account, that they discovered her client had other property, that she is trying to keep communication open, that her intent is to file withdrawal of attorney by the end of the year and she will either file a petition for final distribution or withdrawal of attorney. The status on close of estate is continued to December 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone for Willie Faye Garrett.

Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that she submitted a declaration on Thursday, that she has not heard from her Client, that she spoke with Ms. Garrett's family members and that she has sent letters. Ms. Davis-Jenkins also stated that she has yet to receive anything from the Client giving them authority to finalize the accounting and that her office is preparing to withdraw next week. The Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is off calendar. Status on filing of petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for October 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Sep-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone for Willie Garrett.

Ms. Jenkins stated that her Client discovered two additional properties, that they procured a Buyer for the plots and that they have had a difficult time with the Clearlake property. Ms. Jenkins also stated that they found out that the Realtors they were working with were not qualified to sell the Clearlake land, that they have found a new Realtor, that they have begun the paper work to have the land sold and that she thinks she can amend the petition. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to September 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for amending the petition. The deadline for Ms. Jenkins to respond to the Examiner's notes is August 19, 2019.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Jenkins appeared by telephone for Willie Garrett.

Ms. Jenkins stated that she filed something in response to the Probate Examiner's notes on March 21st, that the accounting was going to be approved, but her Client has discovered other property (land in Clearlake, two burial plots, stocks), that the plots need to be sold and that land is going to take longer than usual to sell. Ms. Jenkins also stated that the assets need to be liquidated and requested a 60 to 90 day continuance to file an amended petition. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone for Willie Faye Garrett.

The Court noted that the response to the Probate Examiner's notes was filed on Friday, but not in time to be reviewed. Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that she thinks the response cures all of the defects, that notice to the Franchise Tax Board and the California Victim Compensation Board was provided on April 23, 2018, and that Malkia and Helen are not entitled to any notice. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to December 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-01-2018 Continued To Nov-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone for Willie Faye Garrett.

The Court noted that Probate Examiner's notes were sent out on July 25, 2018 and that the matter has been continued three times. Ms. Jenkins stated that everything was taken care of, that they are still waiting for the Heirs to sign the waivers of accounting, that the Heirs are all over 85 years of age and that the Executor is going to fly out to them to get the waivers signed. Ms. Jenkins also stated that the notice of hearing was filed on June 13th, and that notice to the Franchise Tax Board, the Department of Health Care Services, and the California Victim Compensation Board as well as three of the waivers will be filed today. In addition, Ms. Jenkins stated that Helen and Malkia are not Heirs, but they were entitled to notice. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to November 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
